title: Greetings & Politeness
description: Hello, thank you, sorry — and the particles and tones that make them polite.
goals: Greet anyone with สวัสดี — and know when หวัดดี is fine
Use ครับ / ค่ะ / คะ correctly, by gender and by tone
Thank, apologize, and wave it all off with ไม่เป็นไร
Ask and answer how someone is doing — the Thai way

title: Yes, No & Basic Responses
description: Thai has no word for “yes” — learn what it uses instead, and never be stuck for an answer.
goals: Answer yes/no questions the Thai way: echo the verb
Negate anything with ไม่ — one pattern, every verb
Say “I don't understand” and “I don't know” politely
Accept and decline offers without offending anyone

title: Introducing Yourself
description: Names, where you're from, and the pronouns that depend on who's talking.
goals: Say your name with ผม/ฉัน ชื่อ… — no “is” required
Ask names and origins: ชื่ออะไร, มาจากไหน
Build nationalities with คน + place
Put question words in the answer's slot — no inversion

title: Numbers & Counting
description: Count to 10,000, read Thai numerals, and survive any price tag.
goals: Count 0–10,000 and hear prices at speed
Read the Thai numerals ๑ ๒ ๓ on signs and menus
Ask “how much?” and understand the answer in baht
Handle 25, 150, 1,990 — real market and 7-Eleven prices

title: Ordering Food & Drink
description: Order at a street stall like you have done it a hundred times.
goals: Order street-stall staples by their real names
Say “not spicy”, “a little spicy”, and mean it
Ask for the check, a bag, no sugar, extra rice
Name the flavors: sweet, sour, salty, spicy, อร่อย

title: Shopping & Prices
description: How much, too expensive, can you go lower — bargaining with a smile.
goals: Ask prices and counter them politely
Say “too expensive” and “can you lower it?” with the right softeners
Sizes, colors, “do you have…?”
Know when to bargain (market) and when not to (mall)

title: Directions & Getting Around
description: Taxis, the BTS, and asking where things are — and understanding the answer.
goals: Tell a taxi or motorbike driver where you are going
Ask “where is…?” and follow left / right / straight
Name the transport: BTS, songthaew, boat, bus
Read destination signs in Thai script

title: Time & Days
description: Days of the week, today and tomorrow, and the Thai six-hour clock.
goals: The seven days and their colors
Today, tomorrow, yesterday, next week
Tell time the Thai way: โมง, บ่าย, ทุ่ม
Ask “when?” and make simple plans

title: Small Talk & Feelings
description: Hungry, tired, hot — and the small talk that keeps Thailand running.
goals: Say how you feel: hungry, tired, happy, hot
The สบาย ๆ mindset and how to talk about it
Weather talk — the universal opener
Keep a conversation alive with จริงเหรอ and มาก

title: Question Words
description: What, where, when, who, why, how much — the full toolkit, one slot rule.
goals: Master all the question words: อะไร ไหน เมื่อไหร่ ใคร ทำไม เท่าไหร่
Every question uses the same slot rule — no inversion, ever
Tell ไหม yes/no questions apart from open questions
Ask follow-ups that keep a conversation going

title: First Sentences
description: Everything from units 1–10, recombined into full sentences you build yourself.
goals: Build complete sentences from the words you already own
Chain verbs the Thai way: ไป + กิน
Handle real mini-dialogues: order, pay, thank, leave
Graduate from phrases to talking
